page: Microsoft Azure F1als_v7 is an entry-level virtual machine belonging to the Falsv7 server family. It features a dedicated AMD processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This instance is configured with 1 vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server type does not include any local storage, requiring the attachment of external block storage. It is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance remote storage volumes. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance. The hardware profile is characterized by its low memory allocation relative to the single dedicated CPU core, and it lacks any GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F1als_v7 offers a low-resource footprint designed for entry-level workloads where minimal memory is required. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without the performance variability associated with shared-core environments, though the absolute compute capacity is limited by the single-core design. This virtual machine is suitable for lightweight utility tasks, simple microservices, small-scale development or testing environments, and basic background processes that do not demand high memory capacity or local disk I/O.
description: The Microsoft Azure F1als_v7 is an entry-level virtual machine within the Falsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD x86_64 processor. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 1 vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory, representing a low-memory configuration with a 1:2 resource ratio. It features no local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance remote storage volumes. The instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators. Designed for cost-conscious deployments requiring dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of larger memory pools, the F1als_v7 is suited for lightweight utility tasks, small microservices, and basic development or testing environments.

tagline: A low-memory, single-core virtual machine with dedicated AMD compute resources and premium remote storage compatibility for lightweight workloads.
